changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured for clarity
Changed k8s_networkpolicy_monitoring_enabled to k8s_networkpolicy_changes_logged for specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security features related to continuous monitoring. However, slight adjustments in naming can enhance clarity and specificity. The function names should clearly indicate the specific action or configuration being checked.
naming_improvements: k8s_audit_logging_configured instead of k8s_audit_logging_enabled to indicate configuration status
k8s_networkpolicy_changes_logged instead of k8s_networkpolicy_monitoring_enabled to specify logging of changes
title: Continuous Monitoring - Continuous monitoring | risk monitoring ensure risk monitoring is an integral pa
step1_reasoning: The control CA-7(4) focuses on continuous monitoring and risk monitoring, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through audit logging and network policy monitoring. Kubernetes audit logs can track changes and activities within the cluster, similar to cloud monitoring services. NetworkPolicies can be monitored for changes to ensure security configurations remain intact. Admission controllers can log policy changes, ensuring that any modifications to security policies are tracked.
